judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports thoroughly and correctly identify the prior Edgerunner attempt, its revert cause, and ground their sign-off recommendation in that history; Report 2 additionally explicitly confirms that cargobox.yaml is the only deploy config present today (i.e., the serverless config was deliberately removed, not merely absent), which Report 1 never states outright — making Report 2 marginally more complete.

report_1.evidence: This has already been implemented once in this exact repo (commits 454a21f and 597777b) before being reverted (1712565).

report_2.evidence: the repo's git history (454a21f, 597777b, 1712565) shows this exact migration was already attempted under ticket PLAT-31 and reverted.

report_1.evidence: Commit 1712565: "serverless isolates cannot hold the Postgres pool; /reports/monthly exhausted Postgres max_connections in production."

report_2.evidence: git show 1712565 in carelog-api — the revert commit message states plainly: "serverless isolates cannot hold the Postgres pool; /reports/monthly exhausted Postgres max_connections in production... Under normal dashboard load the per-request connections opened faster than Postgres could recycle them and tripped the connection limit ('FATAL: too many connections')."
Since PLAT-31 was reverted, none of this is currently live — deploy/cargobox.yaml is the only deploy config present today (carelog-api/deploy/cargobox.yaml, confirmed by find in step 8).

report_1.evidence: this needs to be a deliberate decision by whoever owns PLAT-31/the on-call or platform team, not a routine reapplication of the reverted commits — per the project documentation principle, "the fact that it was tried before is itself information they need."

report_2.evidence: whoever has institutional memory of the PLAT-31 incident (the commit messages read like a real postmortem — someone on the team lived through this and should weigh in before leadership re-greenlights the same migration for cost reasons alone).
